steps.title: Focus on altered uptake
steps.content: The hypothesis is that kanamycin exposure changes how plants take up metals such as iron and zinc. Evidence for that hypothesis should compare the exposed plants with the control plants and show that exposure is associated with a different amount of those metals.

steps.title: Read the comparison
steps.content: The relevant data compare iron and zinc levels in the two plant groups five days after germination. The exposed plants show lower levels of both named metals than the control plants, which supports the idea that kanamycin affects metal uptake.

steps.title: Reject data misreads
steps.content: Choice A reverses the relationship between iron and zinc levels rather than describing the lower levels in exposed plants. Choice B only says both groups have more than 200 parts per million of both metals, which does not show an exposure effect. Choice C reverses the zinc comparison and ignores iron. Choice D states the full supporting pattern: exposed plants have lower iron and lower zinc than controls. The answer is D.
